Okay, kicking off — production deploy debrief for the eu-west-1 rollout that went sideways this morning.
Right. So at 04:12 UTC the canary in eu-west-1 started returning 502s on about thirty percent of payment-flow traffic.
Thirty percent — was that consistent across all payment endpoints or just card auth?
Card auth specifically. The gateway timeouts were in the migration shim — old schema, new query path.
Did the canary alarms catch it before customer impact?
Alarms fired at 04:14. We had two minutes of degraded checkout before the rollback was even considered.
Two minutes is — that's a lot of lost sessions during European morning peak.
We need to talk about whether we should have rolled back faster, or whether the canary threshold was too loose.
Both, honestly. I think the canary threshold was within spec, but the human-in-the-loop step added five minutes.
Five minutes for an oncall ack. That's the pager-to-action SLA, not a defect.
Sure but in this case the right call was a rollback. We knew within thirty seconds.
Roll back immediately and investigate the migration script after.
Agreed. That's the call going forward — fast rollback, post-mortem the diff.
So decision: we roll back the eu-west-1 deploy, restore last-known-good, and Mike — can you own the migration-script investigation?
Yeah I'll take that. I want to understand whether the schema diff was tested against the old gateway path at all.
Open question — does this mean the payment-flow regression suite is missing a case, or did the case exist and not run?
I genuinely don't know. We need to find out before we re-attempt this deploy.
I can pull the CI history. If the suite ran, we should see it green; if it was skipped, we'll see why.
Good. Tag that as urgent — we shouldn't redeploy until that's clear.
On a related note — canary monitoring itself. Two minutes of impact before the rollback decision is the part that scares me.
We should improve the canary signal so it triggers a rollback recommendation, not just an alert.
Like a tiered thing — alert, recommend, auto-rollback at higher thresholds.
Yes. I'll write that up as a proposal but I don't want to assign it yet — we need product input.
Fine. We should improve canary monitoring is the captured intent — owner TBD.
Moving on. The rolled-back state — are we confirmed stable in eu-west-1 right now?
Confirmed stable as of 05:01 UTC. Card auth latency is back under p95 of two-fifty milliseconds.
And the migration shim is — what — on the old code path everywhere now?
Yes. eu-west-1 is on the rollback. us-east-1 and ap-southeast were never on the new path because we hadn't fanned out yet.
Okay. That's actually fortunate. The blast radius was small.
Yeah, the rolling deploy strategy worked — we just needed to pull faster.
Side topic — Lin asked about whether the auth refactor work is gated on this. It isn't, right?
No, auth refactor is on a different code path. We discussed that two weeks ago in the architecture sync.
Right, that one is going through the new gateway directly, not the migration shim.
Good. So our action items are — Mike on the migration script, me on canary proposal, and we both wait on regression-suite answer before redeploy.
Plus the open question about the payment-flow regression test, which I'll mark as needs-resolution before any redeploy.
How do we want to communicate this externally? Status page is up — should we send a follow-up?
Status page incident is resolved. Customer impact was contained. I'll draft a postmortem by EOD Thursday.
Perfect. Anything else before we wrap?
Same. Thanks both. We'll regroup once Mike has the migration-script analysis.
